name: Chandrayaan-4
description: Chandrayaan-4 is India's planned lunar sample return mission. It aims to demonstrate technologies required to collect lunar material and safely return it to Earth.
engineering_significance: Will demonstrate orbital docking, lunar ascent, autonomous sample collection and Earth return technologies.
future_impact: Will prepare India for advanced lunar exploration and future human missions to the Moon.

objectives.title: Collect Lunar Samples
objectives.info: Retrieve lunar soil and rocks for scientific analysis.

objectives.title: Autonomous Docking
objectives.info: Perform spacecraft docking in lunar orbit.

objectives.title: Sample Return
objectives.info: Safely return collected material back to Earth.

payloads.title: Sampling Arm
payloads.info: Collect lunar soil and rock specimens.

payloads.title: Return Capsule
payloads.info: Carry lunar samples safely back to Earth.

name: Chandrayaan-5 (LUPEX)
description: Chandrayaan-5, also known as LUPEX (Lunar Polar Exploration Mission), is a collaborative mission between ISRO and JAXA aimed at exploring water ice deposits near the Moon's south pole using an advanced rover.
engineering_significance: Demonstrates international collaboration, advanced rover mobility, precision landing and long-duration lunar exploration.
future_impact: Will help scientists understand lunar water resources that could support future human settlements on the Moon.

objectives.title: Search for Water Ice
objectives.info: Investigate permanently shadowed regions near the lunar south pole.

objectives.title: Operate Advanced Rover
objectives.info: Deploy a larger rover capable of travelling longer distances.

objectives.title: Study Lunar Environment
objectives.info: Analyze soil, temperature and surface composition.

payloads.title: Advanced Rover
payloads.info: A next-generation rover jointly developed for extended exploration.

payloads.title: Scientific Instruments
payloads.info: Expected instruments for mineralogy, spectroscopy and environmental studies.

expected_outcomes: Better understanding of lunar water ice

name: Shukrayaan-1
description: Shukrayaan-1 is India's planned Venus exploration mission that will investigate the planet's atmosphere, surface and geological activity.
engineering_significance: Will expand India's planetary exploration programme beyond Mars and the Moon using advanced remote sensing instruments.
future_impact: Expected to improve scientific understanding of Venus and planetary climate evolution.

objectives.title: Study Venus Atmosphere
objectives.info: Analyze atmospheric chemistry and cloud structure.

objectives.title: Surface Mapping
objectives.info: Map Venus using radar and remote sensing.

objectives.title: Climate Research
objectives.info: Understand greenhouse effects and planetary evolution.

payloads.title: Synthetic Aperture Radar
payloads.info: Expected to map Venus through dense clouds.

name: Bharatiya Antariksh Station
description: The Bharatiya Antariksh Station will be India's first independent space station, supporting long-duration human spaceflight, scientific research and advanced space technologies.
engineering_significance: Represents one of India's most ambitious space engineering projects involving modular station design, human life support systems and orbital operations.
future_impact: Will enable continuous Indian presence in space and prepare for future lunar and deep-space missions.

objectives.title: Support Human Spaceflight
objectives.info: Enable astronauts to stay in orbit for extended periods.

objectives.title: Microgravity Research
objectives.info: Conduct scientific experiments impossible on Earth.

objectives.title: Technology Demonstration
objectives.info: Validate systems for future deep-space missions.

payloads.title: Life Support Systems
payloads.info: Maintain a safe and habitable environment for astronauts.

timeline.event: Modules launched and assembled in orbit.
